notes: SHIP v1.14.243 — SCOPE FLOW: mscope DEAD-RENDER FIX + MASTER OWNS JOB BIRTH (UI-logic, body.rd-scoped, no CSS/assets/data-layer; per HANDOFF-SCOPE-FLOW-243.md). PART A — THE BUG. Field report (John, 2026-07-13, DFW-05): Master page -> loaded-master banner -> tap SCOPE A JOB -> NOTHING HAPPENS. Root cause CONFIRMED against live source: mscope_open() renders into deploy_opsHost() (= #wk-deploy under redesign) but NEVER MADE THAT HOST VISIBLE — tapped from pg-master the picker painted into a hidden container on a page the user was not on. Nothing thrown, nothing logged: the exact silent-success-into-a-hidden-node class as the .223 DEPLOY DEAD-RENDER fix, except .223 only patched the nav_restore dispatch (d==='mscope') and NEVER the live tap path. The Command Center's +NEW worked purely by the luck of the user already standing in Work->Deploy. FIX: drill at the CHOKEPOINT (top of mscope_open), rd-gated — showMode('work') then deploy_ensureDeployPanelVisible() (the .223 helper). showMode RE-ADDS .wk-grid (grid-landing default), so the .223 helper must follow it to strip the grid and activate the wk-deploy stab. Deliberately NOT showWorkTab('deploy') — that renders the whole Command Center and nav_pushes d:'command' first (wasted paint + polluted back-stack; back out of the picker must return WHERE THE USER CAME FROM). showMode's showPage() is _navInternalCall-guarded so the drill pushes NO nav state; mscope_open's own d:'mscope' stays the only entry. ⚠️ SPEC UNDERCOUNTED THE BLAST RADIUS: there are SEVEN mscope_open() callers, not the three the handoff listed. Fixing at the chokepoint also repairs TWO MORE dead paths the field report never reached — the blocker sheet's NEW FROM MASTER (L19842) and the handoff sheet's (L20120), both reachable from pages that are not Work->Deploy. PART B — MASTER OWNS JOB BIRTH (John ruling 2026-07-13): the Master is the ONLY place a job is born; Deploy is where jobs LIVE, and Deploy never opens a Master file loader again (redesign only). The empty-scope prompt's CTA is now rd-gated: redesign ROUTES to the Master page ('OPEN MASTER FILE ->'), legacy keeps the in-place picker ('LOAD MASTER' -> mscope_loadMaster) VERBATIM. The prompt copy already said 'The Master is the front door' — now the button agrees with it. ⚠️ DEVIATION FROM SPEC (reported): the handoff specified onclick="showPage('master')". Shipped onclick="rd_openMasterFile()" instead — the CANONICAL .167 door. Reason: the hard rule is ONE DOOR PER FEATURE ('new entry points call the ONE canonical rd_open* function'), and a raw showPage('master') would be a second hand-built entrance to a surface that already has one. It is also strictly better here: rd_openMasterFile adds the defensive master_showSection('file') echo, so we land on the FILE browse (where the LOAD MASTER FILE CTA and the loaded-master banner actually live) rather than whatever section pg-master defaults to. ('master' IS whitelisted in the Ship B4 rd showPage guard — ['cmd','work','ref','master'] — so neither form breaks the no-legacy-page-IDs rule; this is about the door rule, not the guard.) mscope_loadMaster() SURVIVES (legacy CTA + the picker's own RE-LOAD button = 4 live call sites; no dead code). VERIFIED IN A REAL BROWSER (headless Chrome against the built file, both houses — not just static checks, because the whole defect class is invisible to a throw-only instrument): rd, standing on pg-cmd with #wk-deploy NOT visible -> mscope_open() -> pg-work active, .wk-grid stripped, host VISIBLE (1184x225), .msc-wrap painted AND non-zero-size, CTA text 'OPEN MASTER FILE ->' wired to rd_openMasterFile(). CTA click -> lands on pg-master, visible. +NEW regression (already in Work->Deploy, second drill) -> still paints, drill is idempotent. ?legacy=1 -> redesign_isOn() false, drill block INERT (wk-grid untouched), picker paints into #ops-content, CTA still 'LOAD MASTER' -> mscope_loadMaster(), RE-LOAD survives. ZERO console errors or warnings in either house. Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js, CSS braces 3856/3856 (IDENTICAL — this ship adds no CSS), CRLF preserved, three-stamp lockstep .242 -> .243. ?legacy=1 byte-identical in behavior (every change rd-gated). BATCH = 6 (.238 .239 .240 .241 .242 .243) — AT THE CAP. Device-verify covers all six at once: DFW2 c1:002 (heights) + SPARKS s3:175 (heights, NOT dh1:005 — that cab does not exist) + the four SCOPE FLOW guards (Master banner SCOPE A JOB -> picker visible, back -> Master; Work grid -> Deploy -> +NEW -> picker; no master -> OPEN MASTER FILE -> pg-master -> load -> SCOPE A JOB; ?legacy=1 still offers LOAD MASTER in place).
